news.t: Deep Dive: These stocks let you tap into the hot housing market
Homebuilder ETFs rally as housing stays hot
Homebuilder ETFs jump on strong housing starts data
HOMZ - Housing ETF - Cuts Fees To Lowest-Cost In Homebuilding Segment
Housing-market ETFs jump on rosy homebuilder report
This fund is a play for the millennial home renter, and some of its holdings are up big this year

holdings.infoBox: HOMZ is an equity ETF with a total of 100 individual holdings. The top holdings are The Home Depot stock at 3.02%, Lowe's Companies at 2.96%, Taylor Morrison Home at 1.79%, Meritage Homes at 1.67%, and KB Home at 1.60%.

overview.description: The Hoya Capital Housing ETF (HOMZ) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the Hoya Capital Housing 100 index. The fund tracks a tier-weighted index of 100 equities representing the US residential housing industry. HOMZ was launched on Mar 20, 2019 and is issued by Hoya.

data.title: Deep Dive: These stocks let you tap into the hot housing market
data.text: Many home builders' valuations are still low, while their implied upside potential is in the double digits.

data.text: Exchange-traded funds with exposure to residential construction and design stocks gained Friday as most other sectors remained under pressure. The iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF was 1.3% higher mi...

data.title: HOMZ - Housing ETF - Cuts Fees To Lowest-Cost In Homebuilding Segment
data.text: NORWALK, Conn., Aug. 5, 2020 /PRNewswire/ -- Hoya Capital Real Estate ("Hoya Capital") today announced it has reduced the expense ratio for Hoya Capital Housing ETF (NYSE: HOMZ) from 0.45% to 0.30%, e...

data.title: Housing-market ETFs jump on rosy homebuilder report
data.text: Housing exchange-traded funds rallied Wednesday after a blockbuster report on sales of previously-owned homes confirmed there's still strong demand underpinning residential real estate.

data.title: This fund is a play for the millennial home renter, and some of its holdings are up big this year
data.text: Hoya Capital Real Estate started an exchange traded fund last March that its founder calls a macro play on the housing shortage in the United States.
